application.docker_compose_hybrid:
version: '3.8'

services:
  # Main Dashboard Service
  dashboard:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.hybrid
    container_name: cisco-sre-dashboard
    ports:
      - "${DASHBOARD_PORT:-3001}:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL:-postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/cisco_sre_dashboard}
      - PORT=3000
      - AI_ENGINE_URL=http://ai-engine:5001
    volumes:
      - ./logs:/app/logs
      - ./data:/app/data
    depends_on:
      - postgres
      - ai-engine
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - cisco-sre-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:3000/api"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 60s

  # Python AI Engine Service
  ai-engine:
    build:
      context: ./backend/ai-engine
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: cisco-sre-ai-engine
    ports:
      - "${AI_ENGINE_PORT:-5001}:5001"
    environment:
      - FLASK_ENV=production
      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL:-postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/cisco_sre_dashboard}
      - MODEL_PATH=/app/models
    volumes:
      - ./backend/ai-engine/models:/app/models
      - ./logs:/app/logs
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - cisco-sre-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:5001/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 45s

  # PostgreSQL Database
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: cisco-sre-postgres
    ports:
      - "${POSTGRES_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-cisco_sre_dashboard}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-postgres}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
      - ./data/backups:/backups
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - cisco-sre-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # Redis Cache (Optional - for session management and caching)
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: cisco-sre-redis
    ports:
      - "${REDIS_PORT:-6379}:6379"
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD:-cisco123}
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - cisco-sre-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  # Nginx Reverse Proxy (Optional - for production load balancing)
  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: cisco-sre-nginx
    ports:
      - "${NGINX_PORT:-80}:80"
      - "${NGINX_SSL_PORT:-443}:443"
    volumes:
      - ./config/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./config/ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl:ro
      - ./logs/nginx:/var/log/nginx
    depends_on:
      - dashboard
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - cisco-sre-network
    profiles:
      - production

# Network Configuration
networks:
  cisco-sre-network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.20.0.0/16

# Volume Configuration
volumes:
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
    driver_opts:
      type: none
      o: bind
      device: ${DATA_DIR:-./data/postgres}
  
  redis_data:
    driver: local
    driver_opts:
      type: none
      o: bind
      device: ${DATA_DIR:-./data/redis}

# Environment Variables Template
# Copy to .env and customize as needed:
#
# # Database Configuration
# DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:securepassword@localhost:5432/cisco_sre_dashboard
# POSTGRES_DB=cisco_sre_dashboard
# POSTGRES_USER=postgres
# POSTGRES_PASSWORD=securepassword
# POSTGRES_PORT=5432
#
# # Application Ports
# DASHBOARD_PORT=3001
# AI_ENGINE_PORT=5001
# REDIS_PORT=6379
# NGINX_PORT=80
# NGINX_SSL_PORT=443
#
# # Security
# REDIS_PASSWORD=cisco123
# JWT_SECRET=your-jwt-secret-here
#
# # Data Directory
# DATA_DIR=./data
#
# # Environment
# NODE_ENV=production
# FLASK_ENV=production
application.config_package_json:

application.config_tsconfig_json:
{
  "include": ["frontend/src/**/*", "shared-types/**/*", "backend/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "build", "dist", "**/*.test.ts"],
  "compilerOptions": {
    "incremental": true,
    "tsBuildInfoFile": "./node_modules/typescript/tsbuildinfo",
    "noEmit": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "strict": true,
    "lib": ["esnext", "dom", "dom.iterable"],
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "types": ["node", "vite/client"],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./frontend/src/*"],
      "@shared/*": ["./shared-types/*"]
    }
  }
}
application.config_hybrid_config_yaml:
# Hybrid Configuration for Cisco SRE AgenticOps Dashboard
# YAML configuration file for startup/shutdown behavior

# General Configuration
service:
  name: "cisco-sre-dashboard"
  description: "Cisco SRE AgenticOps Intelligence Dashboard"
  version: "1.1.0"
  
# Startup Configuration  
startup:
  # Execution modes: auto, docker, normal
  mode: "auto"
  
  # Port configuration
  ports:
    default: 5000
    docker: 3001
    python_ai: 5001
  
  # Health check settings
  health_check:
    enabled: true
    timeout: 30
    max_attempts: 3
    endpoint: "/api"
  
  # Dependency validation
  dependencies:
    required:
      - "node"
      - "npm"
    optional:
      - "docker"
      - "python3"
    
# Docker Configuration
docker:
  # Docker business container settings
  business_container:
    name: "docker.business"
    auto_start: true
    network: "cisco-sre-network"
  
  # Docker Compose settings
  compose:
    file: "docker-compose.hybrid.yml"
    project_name: "cisco-sre"
    
  # Container settings
  container:
    name: "cisco-sre-dashboard"
    restart_policy: "unless-stopped"
    memory_limit: "2g"
    cpu_limit: "1.0"
    
# Database Configuration
database:
  # Default connection if DATABASE_URL not set
  default_url: "postgresql://localhost:5432/cisco_sre_dashboard"
  
  # Connection pool settings
  pool:
    min: 2
    max: 10
    idle_timeout: 30
    
# Logging Configuration
logging:
  # Log directory (relative to script location)
  directory: "logs"
  
  # Log file retention
  retention:
    startup_logs: 30  # days
    service_logs: 7   # days
    
  # Log levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR
  level: "INFO"
  
  # Verbose mode settings
  verbose:
    enabled: false
    include_timestamps: true
    include_colors: true
    
# Environment Configuration
environment:
  # Node.js settings
  node:
    env: "production"
    max_memory: "2048"
    
  # Python AI Engine settings  
  python:
    virtual_env: "ai-engine/.venv"
    requirements: "ai-engine/requirements.txt"
    
# Service Management
service_management:
  # PID file locations
  pid_files:
    main: ".hybrid-service.pid"
    status: ".hybrid-status"
    legacy_node: "/tmp/sre_node.pid"
    legacy_python: "/tmp/sre_python.pid"
    
  # Shutdown settings
  shutdown:
    graceful_timeout: 30
    force_timeout: 10
    cleanup_temp_files: true
    
# Security Settings
security:
  # File permissions
  file_permissions:
    logs: "0644"
    configs: "0600"
    scripts: "0755"
    
  # Network settings
  network:
    bind_localhost_only: false
    allowed_origins:
      - "http://localhost:*"
      - "http://127.0.0.1:*"

# Development Settings
development:
  # Auto-reload settings
  auto_reload:
    enabled: false
    watch_patterns:
      - "*.js"
      - "*.ts"
      - "*.py"
      
  # Debug settings
  debug:
    enabled: false
    port: 9229
    
# Monitoring Settings
monitoring:
  # Health check endpoints
  health_endpoints:
    frontend: "/api"
    python_ai: "/health"
    
  # Metrics collection
  metrics:
    enabled: true
    interval: 60  # seconds
    
# Backup and Recovery
backup:
  # Configuration backup
  config_backup:
    enabled: true
    location: "config/backups"
    
  # Data backup (if applicable)
  data_backup:
    enabled: false
    schedule: "daily"
